description_wiki: The global COVID-19 pandemic (also known as the coronavirus pandemic), caused by severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2), began with an outbreak in Wuhan, China, in December 2019.

surface_forms: his wife was teaching this class, she asked us each about what we're interested in, and I said I was doing music journalism. I worked on a school newspaper at that time, I was the arts and entertainment editor. And she said, Oh, well, you should talk to my partner, Dave Beck. He works at King FM.
description_wiki: David Daniel Beck (June 16, 1894 – December 26, 1993) was an American labor leader, and president of the International Brotherhood of Teamsters from 1952 to 1957.

description: Jesuit university in Seattle, Washington, USA
description_wiki: Seattle University (Seattle U or SU) is a private Jesuit university in Seattle, Washington, United States.

description: capital and most populous city in France
description_wiki: Paris is the capital and largest city of France, with an estimated city population of 2.04 million in an area of 105.4 km2 (40.7 sq mi), and a metropolitan population of 13.2 million as of January 2026.

description: neighborhood of Seattle, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Belltown is the most densely populated neighborhood in Seattle, Washington, United States, located on the city's downtown waterfront on land that was artificially flattened as part of a regrading project.

description: American orchestra based in Seattle, Washington
description_wiki: The Seattle Symphony is an American orchestra based in Seattle, Washington.

description: public research university in Seattle, Washington, United States
description_wiki: The University of Washington (UW, and informally U-Dub or U Dub) is a public research university in Seattle, Washington, United States.
